matchup: Boston Red Sox @ Kansas City Royals

pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Ranger Suarez projected as a leash-stable, contact-suppression arm with enough inning runway to accumulate 5 strikeouts against Kansas City. The edge was not modeled as a ceiling-whiff eruption, but as floor/runway accumulation.

pitch_notes.sinker: 28 pitches, 1 K, 25% CSW; 90.6 mph avg; 43% zone, 13% chase, 50% strike rate
pitch_notes.curveball: 16 pitches, 2 K, 44% CSW; 75.9 mph avg; best K pitch of outing
pitch_notes.four_seam: 14 pitches, 0 K, 57% CSW; strong called-strike profile but limited whiff conversion
pitch_notes.changeup: 12 pitches, 0 K, 0% CSW; negative contributor to K pathway
pitch_notes.cutter: 11 pitches, 0 K, 9% CSW; highest contact damage cluster, 3 hits and 2 hard-hit balls

failure_diagnosis.primary_failure: Leash/runway failed due to pitch-count inflation and walk pressure before the fifth inning was completed.
failure_diagnosis.secondary_failure: Whiff sustainability was not strong enough to overcome reduced innings. Suarez generated only 7 whiffs on 30 swings and finished with 3 Ks.
failure_diagnosis.command_issue: Strike rate was only 56.8%, with 3 walks and 81 pitches through 4.1 IP.
failure_diagnosis.pitch_specific_issue: Curveball carried the K profile, but changeup and cutter produced minimal CSW and did not support accumulation.
failure_diagnosis.contact_suppression_note: Run prevention was fine at 1 ER, but contact suppression alone did not preserve leash because noncompetitive pitches and walks inflated the count.

model_vs_actual.actual_runway: 4.1 IP hook due to 81 pitches and 3 BB

patch_recommendations: Add LIVE_RUNWAY_ABORT trigger: if strike rate below 58% and pitch count above 75 before completing 5 IP, downgrade all 5+ K tickets sharply.
Add SECONDARY_PITCH_SUPPORT requirement for runway K overs: at least two pitches must carry usable CSW, not just one breaking ball.
Add WALK_PRESSURE_PENALTY for low-strike-rate contact suppressors; weak contact is not enough if free passes erase inning runway.
Modify RUNWAY_K_OVER logic: require projected 5.2+ IP probability to remain live after 2nd/3rd inning checkpoints.
For Ranger Suarez specifically, tag as K-floor volatile when cutter/changeup CSW is weak and sinker chase is low.
notes: This was a clean example of a correct broad concept failing in execution: the model identified a plus-price runway K edge, but overestimated leash stability. Suarez had a playable 28% CSW overall, but it was too concentrated in curveball/four-seam called strikes and not enough true whiff depth. Walk pressure and pitch-count inflation killed the accumulation path.
